app.name: Fin (Intercom)
app.bestFor: The most-cited AI support agent — high autonomous resolution rates with outcome-based pricing on top of Intercom.
app.rankBasis: The only product appearing in all three support-agent roundups here, with Intercom's large review base and the strongest cross-source recognition for outcome-priced autonomous support.
app.pricingPaid: $0.99 per resolved outcome; standalone helpdesk integrations have a minimum commitment such as 50 outcomes/mo, while Intercom seats start separately
pros: high-quality answers and polished deployment workflow
charges for defined outcomes rather than every message
works with Intercom and selected external helpdesks
cons: outcome fees can become expensive at high volume
Intercom seats and other usage charges may apply separately
best experience is tied to Intercom's broader platform

app.name: Zendesk AI Agents
app.bestFor: Best if you already run Zendesk — AI agents grounded in your help center with native ticketing and QA.
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly shortlisted across all three comparison sources and backed by one of the category's largest enterprise helpdesk review bases; native fit for existing Zendesk customers drives rank #2.
app.pricingPaid: Paid Zendesk Suite plus AI usage/add-ons; current public price varies by region and package
pros: deep integration with a mature helpdesk
large app and implementation ecosystem
strong reporting, routing and governance for support teams
cons: AI packaging and total cost are complex
administration can be heavy for small teams
reviewers often cite support and customization learning curves

app.name: Ada
app.bestFor: Enterprise-scale automation for teams handling hundreds of thousands of conversations a year, with strong multilingual coverage.
app.rankBasis: Present in all three enterprise chatbot roundups and established in high-volume automation, but public review and pricing transparency are weaker than Fin or Zendesk.
pros: strong multilingual and enterprise-scale automation
no-code tooling for support teams
robust governance and handoff controls
cons: sales-led pricing and onboarding
best suited to large conversation volumes
reviewers cite setup effort for complex integrations and content

app.name: Tidio (Lyro AI)
app.bestFor: Best for small businesses and Shopify stores wanting affordable AI chat with a genuine free tier.
app.rankBasis: A recurring SMB pick across three comparison sources and one of the few products here with a permanent free helpdesk tier and a broad independent review footprint.
app.pricingFree: Free plan — 50 billable human conversations/mo and basic chatbot flows; 0 Lyro AI conversations
app.pricingPaid: Lyro AI from $39/mo — 50 AI conversations/mo; human-conversation limits are priced separately
pros: easy setup for small businesses
real free tier for live support
combines human chat, flows and AI in one interface
cons: AI and human conversation quotas are separate
cost rises as both traffic and Lyro usage grow
less flexible governance than enterprise platforms

app.name: Sierra
app.bestFor: Purpose-built conversational AI agents for large enterprises that need action execution, not just answers.
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly cited as an enterprise innovator in the two 2026 roundups here, but it has limited public customer-review depth and no transparent self-serve pricing.
features: voice, chat, email and SMS support
pros: designed for complex actions rather than FAQ deflection
strong multichannel and enterprise integration story
outcome optimization extends beyond single conversations
cons: no public pricing or self-serve plan
limited independent review evidence
enterprise implementation and governance requirements are substantial

app.name: Gorgias
app.bestFor: The natural pick for e-commerce and Shopify support, with deep order-aware automation.
app.rankBasis: A recurring ecommerce-specific recommendation with a substantial Shopify-merchant review base; it ranks below general support leaders because its strongest fit is retail.
app.pricingPaid: Starter $10/mo — 50 tickets/mo and 30 included automated AI interactions; overage $0.40/ticket
features: social, chat, email, SMS and voice channels
pros: deep Shopify and ecommerce integrations
pricing is based on volume rather than agent seats
agents can act on orders without switching tools
cons: AI interactions also count as helpdesk tickets
overage and AI charges make total cost harder to forecast
less compelling outside ecommerce

app.name: Decagon
app.bestFor: Enterprise AI concierge agents built for very high conversation volumes and complex resolution flows.
app.rankBasis: Included in both enterprise-agent roundups and associated with high-profile large deployments, but independent review volume and pricing evidence remain sparse.
features: AI agents for chat and voice
pros: supports complex, high-volume service flows
covers voice as well as digital channels
analytics focus on improving agent behavior over time
cons: no self-serve pricing or free plan
limited public user-review evidence
deployment is aimed at enterprise teams with integration resources

skill.name: Chatwoot
skill.bestFor: The recognized leader in open-source customer support (34.7k★) — a self-hostable omnichannel inbox (chat, email, WhatsApp, social) and the de-facto Intercom/Zendesk alternative, with AI assist.
skill.rankBasis: About 34.7k GitHub stars and 8.3k forks plus repeated inclusion in open-source support roundups make Chatwoot the clearest purpose-built OSS helpdesk leader.
skill.pricingPaid: Managed Chatwoot Cloud plans available; self-hosted Community Edition remains free
pros: closest open-source replacement for Intercom or Zendesk
broad channel coverage and active community
self-hosting provides data control
cons: production self-hosting needs ongoing operations
advanced enterprise features are not all in Community Edition
large installations require careful performance tuning

skill.name: Dify
skill.bestFor: The most-starred open-source LLM-app platform (150k★) — build a support bot with RAG and agents grounded in your own product docs. Highest adoption in this list, though general-purpose.
skill.rankBasis: At roughly 150.1k GitHub stars and 23.7k forks, Dify has by far the largest OSS adoption signal here and appears in multiple open-source agent roundups, though it is general-purpose rather than a helpdesk.
skill.pricingPaid: Managed Dify Cloud plans available; model/API usage is billed separately
pros: very large ecosystem and rapid development
fast route to a document-grounded support bot
flexible deployment across cloud, VPC and self-hosting
cons: not a complete ticketing or omnichannel support desk
self-hosted upgrades and workers add operational complexity
model and vector-service costs remain external

skill.name: Rasa
skill.bestFor: The mature conversational-AI framework (21.3k★) for teams that want full control over intent, dialogue, and on-prem deployment — a favorite in regulated industries.
skill.rankBasis: About 21.3k GitHub stars and 4.9k forks, years of production use and continued active maintenance make Rasa the most established code-first conversational framework in this set.
skill.pricingPaid: Enterprise products and support available from Rasa; no public standard rate
pros: fine-grained control over dialogue behavior
strong on-prem and regulated-industry fit
mature framework with extensive integration patterns
cons: steeper engineering and training-data burden than no-code tools
not a ready-made support inbox
production tuning and maintenance require specialist skills

skill.name: Botpress
skill.bestFor: A popular open-source chatbot builder (14.8k★) with a visual flow editor and LLM reasoning — good for custom support conversation flows.
skill.rankBasis: About 14.8k GitHub stars and 2.3k forks plus recurring framework-roundup mentions show strong developer awareness, though the current commercial platform is more cloud-centric than the repository label suggests.
skill.pricingFree: Open-source repository available for self-hosting; current Botpress Cloud also offers a limited free tier
skill.pricingPaid: Botpress Cloud paid tiers available; usage and AI spend vary by plan
pros: approachable visual builder for custom agents
large integration and template ecosystem
good balance of low-code flow control and LLM behavior
cons: open-source and cloud feature sets are not equivalent
usage-based cloud pricing can be difficult to estimate
not a full customer-support ticketing suite

skill.name: Typebot
skill.bestFor: An open-source conversational form/chatbot builder (10.2k★) for embeddable support and lead-capture flows on websites and apps.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 10.2k GitHub stars and 3.2k forks plus open-source chatbot-roundup coverage make it a well-adopted visual builder, but its core strength is guided forms rather than autonomous support.
skill.pricingPaid: Managed Typebot Cloud plans available; self-hosting remains free
pros: excellent for deterministic guided conversations
polished builder and embeddable experience
self-hostable with a large community
cons: not designed as an autonomous support agent
no native full helpdesk or agent inbox
complex branching flows become difficult to maintain

skill.name: composio-community/support-skills
skill.bestFor: The only support-specific Agent Skill set (Apache 2.0) — SKILL.md skills for ticket triage, SLA tracking, sentiment detection, and reply drafting. Very low adoption (~13★), so ranked last at low confidence.
skill.rankBasis: Only about 13 GitHub stars and 2 forks make this a very early project; it ranks last despite being the sole repository here focused specifically on reusable support-agent skills.
pros: support-specific workflows rather than a generic agent framework
small, inspectable SKILL.md modules
useful reference patterns for agent teams
cons: extremely limited adoption and independent validation
requires an agent runtime and connected support tools
not a deployable helpdesk or chatbot on its own

notes: This track was rebuilt to fix a ranking bug where all skills sorted above all repos regardless of quality — a 13-star personal skill (composio-community/support-skills) previously ranked #1 above Chatwoot and Dify. Now everything is ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption; kind (skill|repo) is only a tag. The recognized open-source support leaders lead: Chatwoot (34.7k★, the purpose-built Intercom/Zendesk alternative) at #1, then Dify (150k★, build a doc-grounded bot), Rasa (21.3k★), Botpress (14.8k★), and Typebot (10.2k★). Dedicated customer-support Agent Skills barely exist — composio-community/support-skills is the only one and it is tiny (~13★), so it now ranks last. No MCP servers. Star counts verified on GitHub. App track is contaminated by vendor blogs (Fin.ai, eesel, Certainly, TeamSupport all sell competing products); app rankings favor tools recurring across multiple independent lists.
